principles: Use gpt-5.5 for complex reasoning, coding, tool-heavy agents, long-context synthesis, architecture, implementation, and review.
Use gpt-5.4 when the task is still professional coding work but the quality risk is lower and cost/latency matters.
Use gpt-5.4-mini or gpt-5.4-nano only for simple high-volume routing, extraction, classification, ranking, summarization, or cheap subagent work outside the main production worker loop.

Start with medium reasoning for balanced quality, then raise to high for architecture, implementation, review, and hard debugging. Reserve xhigh/pro-class models for asynchronous evals or exceptional hard tasks.

default.why: Default to the current flagship for complex reasoning and coding.

planner.why: Owns decomposition, dependency structure, success criteria, and worker prompts.

divergent.why: Explores options and rejects unsafe routes.

requirements.why: Turns product intent into precise acceptance gates.

uiux.why: Needs visual, product, and existing-system consistency.

prototype.why: Converts requirements and style contracts into an implementable design.

assetgen.why: Image generation now uses local MCP prompt-template retrieval before handing a focused prompt to the raster backend; gpt-5.4-mini is sufficient for this bounded adapter step.

fullstack.why: Highest blast radius: code edits, integration, and architecture preservation.

tester.why: Runs verification and targeted fixes with lower creative/architecture risk.

reviewer.why: Needs strong bug finding, regression analysis, and quality judgment.

closer.why: Audits artifacts and summarizes closure after work is done.

manual_overrides.CODEX_MODEL: Overrides the selected model for emergency or experiment runs.
manual_overrides.CODEX_REASONING_EFFORT: Overrides the selected reasoning effort for emergency or experiment runs.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_MODEL_POLICY: Set to off to disable taskctl model routing.
manual_overrides.TASKCTL_MODEL_POLICY_PATH: Path to an alternate JSON policy file.
